task_completion.description: Implement a .dockerignore file to exclude test files and __pycache__
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output of 'cat .dockerignore' or a text editor showing a .dockerignore file containing entries for '__pycache__' and test directories/files.

task_completion.description: Modify the Dockerfile to exclude dev/test dependencies from the production image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the modified Dockerfile. Look for a multi-stage build pattern or explicit exclusion of dev dependencies like pytest, black, and locust.

task_completion.description: Build the Docker image and tag it as 'acme-analytics:optimized'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of a 'docker build' command with the '-t acme-analytics:optimized' flag, followed by successful build output.

task_completion.description: Ensure the final image size is under 400MB
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the output of 'docker images' or 'docker image ls' where the 'acme-analytics:optimized' image has a value in the 'SIZE' column that is less than 400MB.

task_completion.description: Ensure a second build with unchanged dependencies completes in under 60 seconds utilizing layer caching
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a second 'docker build' execution that completes very quickly (under 60s), with build steps explicitly showing 'CACHED' for the dependency installation layers.

task_completion.description: Start the application and verify the /health endpoint responds correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the container running (e.g., via 'docker run' and 'docker ps') and a successful 'curl' request to the /health endpoint (e.g., curl http://localhost:8001/health or 8000) returning a valid HTTP response.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed Docker commands rather than spoofing outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Docker CLI output formats, build progress bars, and realistic layer hashes in the terminal, rather than simple echoed text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent modified the Dockerfile and .dockerignore using standard tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of text editors (nano, vim) or shell commands (echo, sed) modifying the files in the correct project directory (~/projects/analytics-service/).

integrity.description: Verify the health endpoint response comes from the actual running container
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent runs the container using 'docker run' with appropriate port mappings before executing the 'curl' command, rather than just mocking a local server.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project directory: /home/ga/projects/analytics-service. Target image tag: acme-analytics:optimized. Target image size: < 400MB. Target cached build time: < 60 seconds. Dev dependencies to exclude: pytest, black, locust. Health endpoint verification: The verifier script tests http://localhost:8001/health (host port 8001 mapped to container port 8000).
